title: Coilostylis pugioniformis
intro: Coilostylis pugioniformis, also known as 'Bhoomikhar', is a medicinal plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties, which are said to aid in the treatment of various ailments. Bioactive constituents of Coilostylis pugioniformis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, decoctions, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into Ayurvedic and Unani formulations.
taxonomy: Coilostylis pugioniformis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a member of the genus Coilostylis, which is part of the Apocynaceae family. However, some sources may group it under the Rubiaceae family. Coilostylis pugioniformis is native to Southeast Asia. The classification of this species may be subject to variation depending on the source.
